goal: Generate a 200-frame Synthetic Data Generation (SDG) dataset across 4 cameras (left, right, overhead, front) with CORRELATED domain randomization on lighting parameters and INDEPENDENT per-scene object pose randomization. Scene: 4 labelled cubes on a table under a randomized DomeLight, observed simultaneously by 4 cameras with overlapping fields of view. configure_correlated_dr declares a single parameter group {intensity, color_r, color_g, color_b} with method='copula' and correlation=0.85 so that the four lighting axes vary together (a 'warm dim' frame stays warm and dim across all 4 cameras; a 'cool bright' frame stays cool and bright across all 4 cameras). sample_correlated_dr draws one synchronized lighting sample per frame and applies it BEFORE the four render products step, so every camera observes the same illuminant on the same frame. add_domain_randomizer applies per-frame OBJECT pose jitter (uncorrelated with lighting, but consistent across cameras within a frame because the scene is shared). configure_coco_yolo_writer registers all 4 cameras with id_offset namespacing so per-camera image-IDs do not collide. create_sdg_pipeline + export_dataset writes the 200-frame batch (200 frames × 4 cameras = 800 images). validate_annotations cross-checks COCO output for bounding-box integrity AND per-camera distribution (each camera should produce ≈200 frames). SUCCESS: /tmp/sdg_multi_cam/annotations/instances.json contains entries from all 4 camera namespaces (image-id offsets 1_000_000, 2_000_000, 3_000_000, 4_000_000) AND validate_annotations reports zero bounding-box bound violations across ≥30 sampled frames. Exercises create_prim, apply_api_schema, set_semantic_label, configure_camera (×4 with overlapping FOV), set_camera_params, configure_correlated_dr, sample_correlated_dr, add_domain_randomizer (pose), configure_coco_yolo_writer (multi-camera namespacing), create_sdg_pipeline, preview_sdg, export_dataset, validate_annotations. Realizes sdg-multi-cam-correlated-dr-001 (sdg category, tier-2-locally-runnable).
thoughts: Pattern: other (multi-camera correlated SDG — no robot motion, no pick-place delivery criterion; success is shape of the output dataset and per-frame correlation of lighting metadata).

Four cameras rationale: left (-x perspective), right (+x perspective), overhead (top-down), front (-y eye-level). All four FOVs intersect on the table centre, so each frame produces 4 views of the same scene state. This is the canonical multi-view stereo / NeRF training-data shape. Three cameras would suffice topologically but four matches the backlog spec and gives one orthogonal pair (left/right) + one bird's-eye + one human-eye-level — the standard rig for sim-to-real bin-picking.

CORRELATED DR semantics: configure_correlated_dr.parameter_groups = [{params: ['intensity','color_r','color_g','color_b'], ranges: {intensity: [400, 2200], color_r: [0.7, 1.0], color_g: [0.7, 1.0], color_b: [0.7, 1.0]}, correlation: 0.85, method: 'copula'}]. A Gaussian copula with rho=0.85 means a 'high intensity' frame is highly correlated with a 'warm color' frame (or whatever the joint distribution shape is). The correlation budget is shared across the 4 axes by the copula construction.

Per-frame sync mechanism: sample_correlated_dr(preset='sensor_camera', n_samples=200, seed=42) draws 200 joint samples ONCE. Each frame index k consumes sample k and applies it to /World/DomeLight BEFORE the 4 render products step. Because all 4 cameras step within the SAME orchestrator.step() call, they all see the same illuminant for frame k — this is the 'correlated across cameras' axis that the backlog calls out.

Pose DR is INDEPENDENT per frame (add_domain_randomizer randomizer_type='pose') but CONSISTENT across the 4 cameras of a single frame, because all 4 render products observe the same scene state at the same simulation time. So the multi-view correspondence is preserved frame-by-frame even as objects jitter — exactly what multi-view object-detection training needs.

configure_coco_yolo_writer cameras=4 → handler must produce a SINGLE merged COCO instances.json with per-camera image-IDs namespaced by id_offset. id_offset=1000000 (default) means camera_0 gets image_ids 1_000_000..1_000_199, camera_1 gets 2_000_000..2_000_199, etc. The shared category map (categories=['cube']) is preserved so a downstream YOLOv8 ingestor sees one class across all cameras.

create_sdg_pipeline(annotators=['rgb','bounding_box_2d'], output_format='coco', num_frames=200) registers ONE pipeline that fans out to all 4 cameras. Total image count = 200 × 4 = 800. The pipeline must respect the writer's camera list — passing num_frames=200 should yield 200 multi-camera frames, NOT 50 (the handler must NOT collapse to num_frames/num_cameras).

preview_sdg(num_samples=4) generates ONE preview frame per camera so the operator can confirm lighting is synchronized (all 4 previews should share the same illuminant warmth/intensity) before committing to the full 200-frame run.

validate_annotations(num_samples=30) reads back the merged COCO file and confirms: (a) bounding boxes within image bounds (per-image, since cameras have different intrinsics), (b) no zero-area boxes, (c) each declared class ('cube') is represented in samples from each camera namespace.

Distinct from CP-NEW-scene-sdg-full-pipeline (single camera, 4-axis DR, 200 frames). Distinct from CP-NEW-sdg-class-balance-rebalance (single camera, multi-class with retry loop). Distinct from CP-NEW-multi-cam-triangulation (geometric calibration, no DR, no COCO output). This canonical is the ONLY one that combines correlated-DR + multi-camera namespacing + synchronized illuminant application within a single SDG run.
motion_controllers: none — pure data generation, no arm motion in this workflow.

BLOCKER: omni.replicator must be enabled. configure_correlated_dr + sample_correlated_dr handlers must be present in the running build (Phase 7B SDG addendum). configure_coco_yolo_writer with len(cameras)>1 must be tested — if the writer collapses multi-camera output into a single namespace, image-IDs collide and the COCO file is corrupted.
code:
# ── Scene skeleton ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1200.0)
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:color", value=[1.0, 1.0, 1.0])
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

# ── Table surface ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Table", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, 0.375], scale=[1.0, 0.7, 0.375])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# ── 4 labelled cubes arranged on the table ────────────────────────────────
for i in range(4):
    col = i % 2
    row = i // 2
    path = f"/World/Cube_{i+1}"
    create_prim(
        prim_path=path,
        prim_type="Cube",
        position=[col * 0.20 - 0.10, row * 0.20 - 0.10, 0.81],
        size=0.07,
    )
    for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI"):
        apply_api_schema(prim_path=path, schema_name=api)
    set_semantic_label(prim_path=path, class_name="cube")

# ── 4 cameras with overlapping FOV around the table centre (0,0,0.75) ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
_camera_specs = [
    ("/World/Cam_Left",     [-1.20,  0.0,  0.95], [0, 0, 0.75]),
    ("/World/Cam_Right",    [ 1.20,  0.0,  0.95], [0, 0, 0.75]),
    ("/World/Cam_Overhead", [ 0.0,   0.0,  1.80], [0, 0, 0.75]),
    ("/World/Cam_Front",    [ 0.0,  -1.20, 0.95], [0, 0, 0.75]),
]
for cam_path, cam_pos, cam_look in _camera_specs:
    configure_camera(
        camera_path=cam_path,
        focal_length=35.0,
        horizontal_aperture=20.955,
        vertical_aperture=15.2908,
        clipping_range=[0.01, 10.0],
    )
    # Position is set on the prim transform; set_camera_params handles intrinsics only.
    set_attribute(prim_path=cam_path, attr_name="xformOp:translate", value=cam_pos)
    set_camera_params(
        camera_path=cam_path,
        params={"focal_length": 35.0, "horizontal_aperture": 20.955, "vertical_aperture": 15.2908},
    )

# ── Correlated DR on lighting (intensity + RGB color jointly sampled) ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
configure_correlated_dr(
    parameter_groups=[
        {
            "params": ["intensity", "color_r", "color_g", "color_b"],
            "ranges": {
                "intensity": [400.0, 2200.0],
                "color_r":   [0.70, 1.00],
                "color_g":   [0.70, 1.00],
                "color_b":   [0.70, 1.00],
            },
            "correlation": 0.85,
            "method": "copula",
        }
    ],
    target_path="/World/DomeLight",
    seed=42,
)

# Draw 200 synchronized lighting samples up front (one per frame; same draw applies to all 4 cameras of that frame)
_lighting_samples = sample_correlated_dr(
    axes=[
        {"name": "intensity", "mean": 1300.0, "std": 450.0},
        {"name": "color_r",   "mean": 0.85,   "std": 0.08},
        {"name": "color_g",   "mean": 0.85,   "std": 0.08},
        {"name": "color_b",   "mean": 0.85,   "std": 0.08},
    ],
    correlations=[
        {"axis_a": "intensity", "axis_b": "color_r", "rho": 0.85},
        {"axis_a": "intensity", "axis_b": "color_g", "rho": 0.85},
        {"axis_a": "intensity", "axis_b": "color_b", "rho": 0.85},
    ],
    n_samples=200,
    seed=42,
    name="multi_cam_lighting",
)

# ── Per-frame OBJECT pose jitter (independent of lighting; consistent across the 4 cameras of a frame) ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
add_domain_randomizer(
    target="/World/Cube_.*",
    randomizer_type="pose",
    params={"min_angle": -0.40, "max_angle": 0.40, "surface_prim": "/World/Table"},
)

# ── Configure multi-camera COCO writer (per-camera image-ID namespacing via id_offset) ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
configure_coco_yolo_writer(
    output_dir="/tmp/sdg_multi_cam",
    cameras=[
        "/World/Cam_Left",
        "/World/Cam_Right",
        "/World/Cam_Overhead",
        "/World/Cam_Front",
    ],
    format="coco",
    categories=["cube"],
    id_offset=1000000,
)

# ── Build SDG pipeline (200 multi-camera frames → 800 images total) ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_sdg_pipeline(
    annotators=["rgb", "bounding_box_2d"],
    output_format="coco",
    num_frames=200,
    output_dir="/tmp/sdg_multi_cam",
    camera_position=[0, 0, 1.80],
    camera_look_at=[0, 0, 0.75],
    resolution=[1280, 720],
)

# ── Preview: 4 frames (one per camera) to verify lighting is synchronized across all 4 ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
preview_sdg(num_samples=4)

# ── Full export: 200 multi-camera frames → 800 annotated images ──────────────────────────────────────
export_dataset(
    output_dir="/tmp/sdg_multi_cam",
    num_frames=200,
    step_batch=10,
)

# ── Validate annotations: bbox bounds + per-camera coverage ──────────────────────────────────────────────
validate_annotations(num_samples=30)
verified_status: drafted-2026-05-16-A80; form-gate-schema TBD; function-gate BLOCKED (requires omni.replicator extension + Phase 7B SDG addendum handlers for configure_correlated_dr, sample_correlated_dr, configure_coco_yolo_writer with multi-camera namespacing)
failure_modes: omni.replicator not enabled: enable_extension('omni.replicator.core') must be called before create_sdg_pipeline; the handler may auto-enable it but Kit must have been launched with the extension available

configure_correlated_dr parameter_groups: a typo in params (e.g. 'color_R' vs 'color_r') silently drops that axis from the correlated set, so it falls back to independent uniform sampling and the correlation guarantee is violated WITHOUT raising — verify with sample_correlated_dr inspection before exporting

sample_correlated_dr n_samples < num_frames: if fewer samples than frames are drawn, the export loop reuses samples and the 'one synchronized sample per frame' invariant breaks. n_samples MUST equal num_frames (200 here)

configure_coco_yolo_writer cameras list ordering: the order of the cameras list determines id_offset multiplier (cam[0]→1×offset, cam[1]→2×offset, etc.). If a downstream consumer expects a specific camera→ID mapping (e.g. 'overhead is always namespace 3'), the cameras list MUST be ordered to match — reordering after the fact corrupts ID continuity

configure_coco_yolo_writer multi-camera collapse bug: some writer implementations attach to only the FIRST camera and silently drop the rest. Verify by checking that instances.json contains images with image_id >= 2_000_000 (cam_2 namespace); if all images have id < 2_000_000 only one camera was actually written

create_sdg_pipeline num_frames semantics: handler may interpret num_frames as 'frames per camera' (yielding 800 total images) OR 'total images' (yielding 200 ÷ 4 = 50 per camera). The diagnose_args assume per-frame×4-cameras semantics; if the handler diverges adjust num_frames to 200 explicitly per camera or num_frames=50 if total-images semantics apply

add_domain_randomizer pose surface_prim='/World/Table': Table must have PhysicsCollisionAPI so PhysX can report its surface; missing API causes pose jitter to project cubes through the table and out of all 4 camera FOVs simultaneously

configure_camera vs set_camera_params: configure_camera is marked DEPRECATED in the handler — set_camera_params is the canonical path. Both are called here for backward compat, but if the deprecated handler is removed, only set_camera_params will set intrinsics. xformOp:translate is set via set_attribute to position the camera

preview_sdg(num_samples=4) with 4 cameras: handler may produce 4 frames total (1 per camera at the same time-step) OR 4 frames per camera (16 total). The lighting-sync verification needs the former; if the latter is observed, the previews still verify sync but the visual grid is larger

validate_annotations num_samples=30 across 800 images: with random sampling, ~7-8 samples land in each camera's namespace. The 'each declared class present in each camera namespace' check needs all 4 namespaces to be sampled at least once — if only 2 cameras' worth of samples are drawn, raise num_samples to 60 to increase per-camera coverage
Output dir /tmp/sdg_multi_cam must be writable; 800 frames at 1280x720 RGB ≈ 240 MB. On shared servers /tmp may be small — use workspace/sdg_output/multi_cam/ if /tmp fills

configure_correlated_dr.target_path='/World/DomeLight': the correlated DR sample is applied to a single light. If the scene later adds a second light (e.g. a key/fill rig), the second light's intensity is NOT in the correlated set and breaks the multi-camera lighting-sync invariant — single-DomeLight scenes only for this canonical
extension_notes: First canonical in library to combine configure_correlated_dr + multi-camera configure_coco_yolo_writer + per-frame synchronized lighting application within a single SDG run. CP-NEW-scene-sdg-full-pipeline is single-camera and uses independent (not correlated) DR. CP-NEW-multi-cam-triangulation uses multiple cameras for geometric calibration but no DR and no COCO export. CP-NEW-dr-curriculum has a configure_correlated_dr call but uses speculative arg names (correlation_groups instead of parameter_groups) and only one camera. This template uses the verified _models.py signature (parameter_groups: List[Dict]) and exercises the multi-camera-ID namespacing path of configure_coco_yolo_writer. Backlog source: sdg-multi-cam-correlated-dr-001 (priority_tier=2, category=sdg, tier=tier-1-locally-runnable, blockers=[]). Note: backlog spec mentions 4 cameras and uses 'seed-shared lighting' as the correlation construct; this template implements that as a Gaussian copula with rho=0.85 over {intensity, color_r, color_g, color_b}, which is a stronger guarantee than seed-sharing (a seed-shared sampler could still produce uncorrelated marginals if drawn independently). If seed-sharing is the desired construct instead, replace configure_correlated_dr with add_domain_randomizer + a shared seed parameter — but the current implementation matches the 'correlated DR for stereo/multi-view consistency' notes in the backlog description.
code_template:
create_prim(prim_path={{scene_light.path}}, prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path={{scene_light.path}}, attr_name="inputs:intensity", value={{scene_light.intensity}})
set_attribute(prim_path={{scene_light.path}}, attr_name="inputs:color", value={{scene_light.color}})
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

create_prim(prim_path={{scatter_surface.path}}, prim_type="Cube", position={{scatter_surface.position}}, scale={{scatter_surface.scale}})
apply_api_schema(prim_path={{scatter_surface.path}}, schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

for wp in {{scene_objects}}:
    create_prim(prim_path=wp["path"], prim_type="Cube", position=wp["position"], size=0.07)
    for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI"):
        apply_api_schema(prim_path=wp["path"], schema_name=api)
    set_semantic_label(prim_path=wp["path"], class_name="cube")

for cam in {{cameras}}:
    configure_camera(
        camera_path=cam["path"],
        focal_length=35.0,
        horizontal_aperture=20.955,
        vertical_aperture=15.2908,
        clipping_range=[0.01, 10.0],
    )
    set_attribute(prim_path=cam["path"], attr_name="xformOp:translate", value=cam["position"])
    set_camera_params(
        camera_path=cam["path"],
        params={"focal_length": 35.0, "horizontal_aperture": 20.955, "vertical_aperture": 15.2908},
    )

configure_correlated_dr(
    parameter_groups=[
        {
            "params": ["intensity", "color_r", "color_g", "color_b"],
            "ranges": {"intensity": [400.0, 2200.0], "color_r": [0.70, 1.00], "color_g": [0.70, 1.00], "color_b": [0.70, 1.00]},
            "correlation": 0.85,
            "method": "copula",
        }
    ],
    target_path={{scene_light.path}},
    seed=42,
)

_lighting_samples = sample_correlated_dr(
    axes=[
        {"name": "intensity", "mean": 1300.0, "std": 450.0},
        {"name": "color_r",   "mean": 0.85,   "std": 0.08},
        {"name": "color_g",   "mean": 0.85,   "std": 0.08},
        {"name": "color_b",   "mean": 0.85,   "std": 0.08},
    ],
    correlations=[
        {"axis_a": "intensity", "axis_b": "color_r", "rho": 0.85},
        {"axis_a": "intensity", "axis_b": "color_g", "rho": 0.85},
        {"axis_a": "intensity", "axis_b": "color_b", "rho": 0.85},
    ],
    n_samples=200,
    seed=42,
    name="multi_cam_lighting",
)

add_domain_randomizer(
    target="/World/Cube_.*",
    randomizer_type="pose",
    params={"min_angle": -0.40, "max_angle": 0.40, "surface_prim": {{scatter_surface.path}}},
)

configure_coco_yolo_writer(
    output_dir="/tmp/sdg_multi_cam",
    cameras=[cam["path"] for cam in {{cameras}}],
    format="coco",
    categories=["cube"],
    id_offset=1000000,
)

create_sdg_pipeline(
    annotators=["rgb", "bounding_box_2d"],
    output_format="coco",
    num_frames=200,
    output_dir="/tmp/sdg_multi_cam",
    camera_position=[0, 0, 1.80],
    camera_look_at=[0, 0, 0.75],
    resolution=[1280, 720],
)

preview_sdg(num_samples=4)

export_dataset(
    output_dir="/tmp/sdg_multi_cam",
    num_frames=200,
    step_batch=10,
)

validate_annotations(num_samples=30)
